rationale: All four acceptance criteria pass. The primary defect — installVersion() discarding the verified version causing stale getVersion() reads — is correctly fixed across all affected callers. TypeScript compiles cleanly; 149 unit test files pass. The edge-case lens found that setup.ts still carries the same bug pattern (EC-1), but the spec explicitly scopes this fix to the install command spinner surface only (deferred). The acceptance lens noted a kimi spec-table deviation (result.installedVersion vs. getVersion()) that is functionally equivalent or better since installNativeAgent already runs verifyInstallation internally (deferred as spec doc update). Test diff artifact was incomplete but both test files confirmed present and correct on disk. 1 deferred issue, 0 dismissed false-positives, 0 blocking findings.

business_review.text: codemie install claude 2.1.34 over existing 2.1.33 reports Claude Code v2.1.34 installed successfully
business_review.evidence: install.ts captures installVersion() return; claude.plugin.ts returns result.installedVersion ?? null; install.version-selection.test.ts line 109 asserts exactly this scenario.

business_review.text: Downgrade scenario (2.1.34 → 2.1.33) reports the correct lower version
business_review.evidence: Data flow is symmetric — displayVersion sourced from result.installedVersion with no comparison against previous version. Mechanism identical to upgrade path covered by AC-1 test.

business_review.text: When post-install verification produces no version, success message omits version number rather than showing stale one
business_review.evidence: install.ts: displayVersion ? ` v${displayVersion}` : '' guards the version string. install.version-selection.test.ts lines 112-143 test the null-fallback path explicitly.

business_review.text: No regressions in update or setup flows that also call installVersion()
business_review.evidence: Neither update.ts nor setup.ts appears in the diff; both discard the return value which is backward-compatible. kimi install() correctly changed from return to await to preserve Promise<void>.

standards_review.notes: Commit uses fix(cli): scope; 'cli' is in the project's allowed-scopes list. Commit message is clear and references the ticket.

TypeScript compiles cleanly (tsc --noEmit). 149 unit test files pass. No any, no unsafe casts introduced. The `return await` style in async functions is a minor redundancy (not a blocking quality issue).
shell: true added to exec() in ClaudePlugin.getVersion() for a hardcoded binary path (~/.local/bin/claude), not user input. No injection risk. No new attack surface introduced.
